skill.name: openapi-spec-generation
skill.description: Generate and maintain OpenAPI 3.1 specifications from code, design-first specs, and validation patterns. Use when creating API documentation, generating SDKs, or ensuring API contract compliance.
skill.summary: Generate and maintain OpenAPI 3.1 specifications from code, design-first specs, and validation patte...
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer found command, URL, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection, but the skill includes legitimate install and CLI command examples that may modify the developer environment.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate External Tool Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill provides bash examples that install npm packages globally and run OpenAPI validation, bundling, preview, and SDK generation tools. These commands are relevant to the skill, but they can change the local environment or write generated files if executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct and executable, but they match the documented purpose of OpenAPI linting and SDK generation. I found no evidence that they exfiltrate data or run hidden payloads.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Example and Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The URL alerts are examples, local development addresses, and links to public OpenAPI-related documentation. They do not send credentials or make network requests by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visibly documentation links or placeholder API server values. No surrounding code performs outbound transmission.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts match OpenAPI security terms such as bearer authentication, JWT format, API key schemas, and security sections. I found no cryptographic implementation or weak hash algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keyword review found security documentation, not crypto code. The file contains no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, custom encryption, or password handling logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts are caused by ordinary API schema identifiers, UUID formats, and response examples. These lines document API shapes and do not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is OpenAPI and tsoa documentation syntax. I found no commands that enumerate files, users, networks, processes, or environment details.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment-Modifying CLI Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows global npm installation and generator commands. Users should review package sources and output paths before allowing an agent to run them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is clearly present and can affect a local machine. It is still normal for this type of API tooling skill.

content.value_statement: API teams need accurate contracts for documentation, validation, and SDK generation. This skill provides OpenAPI 3.1 patterns, templates, and tooling guidance.
actual_capabilities: Creates OpenAPI 3.1 structures with paths, schemas, responses, and security schemes.
Provides design-first and code-first patterns for REST API documentation.
Shows FastAPI and tsoa examples for generating specifications from code.
Defines validation and linting workflows using Spectral and Redocly.
Describes SDK generation commands for TypeScript, Python, and Go clients.
limitations: Does not inspect a live API unless the agent receives project files or endpoint details.
Does not guarantee generated SDKs compile without dependency and environment checks.
Uses example domains and schemas that must be replaced for production APIs.
Includes command examples that should be reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Create a New API Contract
use_cases.description: Draft an OpenAPI 3.1 specification before implementation, including paths, request bodies, errors, and authentication.

use_cases.title: Document Existing Services
use_cases.description: Turn FastAPI or TypeScript controller patterns into a consistent OpenAPI specification for internal and external consumers.

use_cases.title: Prepare SDK Generation
use_cases.description: Validate, bundle, and structure an OpenAPI file before producing client libraries for multiple languages.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new REST API contract.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an OpenAPI 3.1 draft for a simple resource API. Include paths, schemas, examples, errors, and bearer authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this OpenAPI file for missing operation IDs, incomplete response coverage, schema reuse, and unclear descriptions.

prompt_templates.title: Generate From Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using code-first API frameworks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this FastAPI or tsoa code to design the matching OpenAPI contract. Highlight gaps between implementation and documented behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Validation Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a validation workflow for this OpenAPI spec using linting, bundling, breaking-change checks, and SDK generation readiness.

output_examples.input: A request to document a user management API.
output: A structured OpenAPI outline with user paths, reusable schemas, response models, and security requirements.
A list of required follow-up details, such as pagination rules, error codes, and authentication behavior.

output_examples.input: A request to prepare a spec for SDK generation.
output: A validation checklist for operation IDs, schema naming, media types, and response examples.
Recommended generator targets and package naming decisions for client libraries.

output_examples.input: A request to improve an existing API contract.
output: A review summary that identifies missing descriptions, inconsistent schemas, and weak error documentation.
Prioritized remediation steps for publication and consumer readiness.

best_practices: Use reusable schemas, parameters, responses, and examples to keep the contract consistent.
Define authentication, authorization, and error responses for every protected operation.
Run linting and bundling checks before publishing documentation or generating SDKs.
anti_patterns: Do not leave placeholder domains, vague descriptions, or sample-only schemas in production specs.
Do not generate SDKs from specs that have unresolved references or invalid examples.
Do not let agents run install or generation commands without reviewing output paths first.

faq.question: Does this skill create OpenAPI 3.1 specifications?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides templates and patterns for OpenAPI 3.1 paths, schemas, responses, examples, and security schemes.

faq.question: Can it work from existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes code-first examples for FastAPI and TypeScript tsoa patterns.

faq.question: Does it validate specifications automatically?
faq.answer: It provides validation workflows and command examples. The agent still needs permission and the right local tools to run them.

faq.question: Can it generate client SDKs?
faq.answer: It explains how to prepare and run SDK generation for TypeScript, Python, and Go clients.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk notice. The command examples are legitimate, but users should review them before execution.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide API endpoints, request and response models, authentication rules, error formats, and target SDK languages.
